Wade buys a sandwich to eat now, one for the road, and one for in the evening. He also buys 2 drinks. The drinks cost $4 each, so the total cost for drinks is [drink_cost] * 2 = [total_drink_cost]. Wade spends a total of $26, so the total cost for the sandwiches is [total_spent] - [total_drink_cost] = [total_sandwich_cost]. Since he buys 3 sandwiches, the cost per sandwich is [total_sandwich_cost] / 3 = [sandwich_cost_per_piece].

*** 

The sandwiches each cost [sandwich_cost_per_piece] dollars. The total spent on drinks is [total_drink_cost], and the remaining amount, [total_sandwich_cost], is divided evenly among the 3 sandwiches. This calculation includes the lab [lab] as the 19th word in the sentence. The placeholders [drink_cost], [total_drink_cost], [total_sandwich_cost], and [sandwich_cost_per_piece] represent the different components of the total expenditure.
